name: SurrealDB
description: SurrealDB is a multi-model database that unifies document, graph, vector, and relational paradigms into a single engine with a custom query language (SurrealQL). It runs embedded in Rust and Python with zero external dependencies via the local engine (surrealdb::engine::local), supports in-memory and persistent storage backends including RocksDB and SurrealKV, and offers full-text search, geospatial queries, and live query subscriptions. The same engine can also run as a distributed server with WebSocket and HTTP connectivity, making it suitable for local-first apps as well as cloud deployments.
format_on_disk: Multi-file (RocksDB LSM-Tree or SurrealKV SSTables) or in-memory
core_strengths: Multi-model engine unifying document, graph, and vector in one system
Zero-dependency embedded mode via surrealdb::engine::local in Rust
Custom SurrealQL language with graph traversal, subqueries, and live queries
Built-in vector similarity search and full-text search indexing
Same engine runs embedded, single-node server, or distributed cluster
WASM and edge-compatible with cloud sync capability
best_use_cases: Local-first applications needing document, graph, and vector in one DB
AI/ML apps combining vector search with graph relationship traversal
Desktop and edge apps requiring embedded multi-model storage
Real-time collaborative apps using live query subscriptions
Prototyping cloud-native apps locally before scaling to distributed deploy
remarks: SurrealDB v3.x moved from Apache-2.0 to BSL-1.1, converting to Apache-2.0 after the Change Date (2030-01-01). The BSL permits all uses except offering SurrealDB as a Database Service. The Rust crate requires feature flags for storage backends: kv-mem (in-memory), kv-rocksdb (RocksDB), kv-surrealkv (SurrealKV). RocksDB depends on non-Rust libraries. The Python SDK bundles the SurrealDB engine via Rust FFI; embedded mode requires the surrealdb pip package. The SurrealQL language is not standard SQL — it uses a custom syntax with graph traversal operators (->, <-, <->). SurrealDB can also run as a distributed server (surrealdb binary) with WebSocket and HTTP APIs. WASM support is available for browser use.
